title: package-audit configuration
description: Input config for the READ-ONLY npm-package-usage auditor (PROMPT.md §1). Path precedence at runtime: --config <path> > CONFIG_PATH env var > ./config.json.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema. Ignored by the tool (excluded from config_hash).

githubHost.description: Bare host or host:port (port 1-65535; the upper bound is enforced at runtime). Every gh call runs with GH_HOST=<githubHost>; API hostnames are never hand-built (GHES paths differ — gh handles them). null = use the default.

organizations.description: null/omitted = DISCOVERY MODE: enumerate every org the authenticated gh account is a MEMBER of and scan them all. An explicit array restricts scope (recommended for engagements). An explicit [] is 'configured' (not discovery): the effective list is then just the personal namespace when includePersonalNamespace is true, otherwise the run fails fast.

excludeOrganizations.description: Removed from the effective owner list in BOTH modes. null = use the default (none).

includePersonalNamespace.description: Opt-in: also scan the authenticated user's own repos (not an org, so off by default). null = use the default.

includeForks.description: Opt-in: include forked repos (excluded by default so a package's own forks don't double-count). null = use the default.

includeArchived.description: Opt-in: include archived repos. null = use the default.

maxReposPerOrg.description: Per-owner cap applied after sorting repos by pushed_at DESC. null = unlimited.

maxBranchesPerRepo.description: Per-repo cap on after-cutoff branches, newest-commit first. Branches past the cap are recorded as past-cap in the report and exports (visible in scope), but are not scanned for findings this run.

branches.description: Branch-name allowlist — exact name or Bun glob. null/omitted = unrestricted (every branch eligible, subject to cutoff/cap). [] = nothing but the default branch is eligible. A non-empty array restricts eligibility to matching branches. The DEFAULT branch is always eligible regardless of this list. A leading '!' is rejected at runtime (glob negation is not supported). excludeBranches is applied first (deny wins).

excludeBranches.description: Branch-name denylist — exact name or Bun glob. Matching branches are excluded from scanning (recorded as policy-excluded), EXCEPT the default branch, which is always eligible. Deny wins over 'branches'. null = use the default (none). A leading '!' is rejected at runtime.

cutoffDate.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD; must be a real calendar date — enforced at runtime). Branches whose last commit is strictly BEFORE this date are recorded as skipped-cutoff and never inspected.

concurrency.description: Fan-out bounds. Excluded from config_hash (tuning speed never orphans resumable work).

branches.description: Per-repo branch fan-out concurrency (a parallelism limit — unrelated to the root-level 'branches' allow/deny list).

packages.description: The tracked npm packages. Names must be UNIQUE across the array (a name maps to exactly one registry per config).

name.description: The npm REGISTRY name — the single canonical identity for manifest/lockfile matching, API introspection, and reporting. A strict, fail-closed subset of npm names (uppercase allowed; exotic legacy punctuation and traversal/percent/query/fragment chars rejected) so the name can never reshape a registry URL (PROMPT.md §5.E); enforced identically at runtime. npm-alias installs are detected at scan time.

registryUrl.description: Registry BASE URL. Must be https:// and must not contain userinfo (user:pass@) — the pattern rejects an '@' in the authority; full URL validity (parseable, port <= 65535) is enforced at runtime. null = use the default.

registryAuthEnvVar.description: NAME of the env var holding a bearer token for a private registry. The var name participates in config_hash; the token value is never hashed or logged. Must be SET and non-empty at runtime.

excludeDirGlobs.description: Paths matching these globs are never scanned (vendored/generated code). null = use the default.

paths.description: Write locations. Both must resolve under ./data or ./output (write containment, PROMPT.md §0).

sqlitePath.description: SQLite database file (the durable source of truth).

outputDir.description: Where run-<run_id>.json and latest.json are written.
